data.title: Binance Alpha to Feature Dexlab (XLAB), Airdrop 1,500,000 XLAB Tokens to Eligible Users
data.description: Binance announced on X that Binance Alpha will be the first platform to feature Dexlab (XLAB), with Alpha trading opening on August 29, 2025, at 07:00 (UTC).Eligible users can claim an airdrop of 1,500,000 XLAB tokens on the Alpha Events page within 24 hours once trading begins by using Binance Alpha Points.The airdrop will be distributed in two phases:Phase 1 (first 18 hours): Users with at least 254 Binance Alpha Points can claim the airdrop.Phase 2 (last 6 hours): Users with at least 230 Binance Alpha Points can participate in the airdrop on a first-come, first-served basis. If the rewards are not fully distributed, the score threshold will automatically decrease by 15 points every hour.Claiming the airdrop will consume 15 Binance Alpha points and users must confirm their claims on the Alpha Events page within 24 hours, otherwise it will be deemed that users have given up claiming the airdrop.

data.title: Tron Network Implements Significant Fee Reduction
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Tron founder Justin Sun announced that a proposal by the Tron Super Representative community to reduce network fees by 60% has been approved. This marks the largest fee reduction since the network's inception. The new fee structure will take effect on Friday, August 29, at 20:00 UTC+8.

Justin Sun expressed that the fee reduction is a substantial benefit for users, as few networks can afford such a significant decrease. While this move may impact Tron's short-term profitability, Sun believes it will enhance long-term profitability by attracting more users and transactions to the network.

The Tron Super Representative community plans to conduct quarterly reviews of the network's fee structure. These evaluations will consider various factors, including TRX price fluctuations, network usage, and future growth rates, to maintain a balance between profitability and competitiveness.

data.title: 100 Reasons Why DeFi Market Must Be Protected
data.description:
Key Insights: Over 110 Web3 stakeholders, including crypto firms, investors, and advocacy groups, have asked US lawmakers to protect DeFi developers and non-custodial services in new digital asset laws. They warned that blockchain innovation could slow if clear federal rules are not written into future market structure legislation.
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2025/08/29/100-reasons-why-defi-market-must-be-protected/

data.title: Lightning Labs CEO Proposes $200 Million Acquisition Firm
data.description: According to Foresight News, Lightning Labs CEO Ryan Gentry has filed to establish a special purpose acquisition company (SPAC) named Bitcoin Infrastructure Acquisition Corp. The proposed SPAC aims to acquire companies within the digital financial infrastructure sector. The board of directors includes members from DeFi Dev Corp and Nakamoto.

data.title: Sequoia Capital Highlights AI as a Major Cognitive Revolution
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Sequoia Capital has characterized the current wave of artificial intelligence as a profound 'cognitive revolution,' suggesting its transformative power could rival or even surpass the Industrial Revolution, presenting a massive $10 trillion business opportunity. Recently, Sequoia Capital partner Konstantine Buhler delivered a speech titled 'AI Revolution: A $10 Trillion Wave, Greater Than the Industrial Revolution,' emphasizing that the next battleground for AI is the vast service industry.

Buhler noted that similar to how SaaS software not only encroached on traditional software markets but also significantly expanded market boundaries, AI is poised to disrupt and expand the service sector. This includes traditional service industries like law, accounting, and healthcare, which have been dominated by large partnership firms. AI is expected to drive the emergence of new publicly traded companies and potentially alter the familiar S&P 500 index.

At the dawn of this 'cognitive revolution,' Sequoia Capital is actively seeking and investing in startups capable of 'specializing' general AI technologies. These companies are likened to the Rockefellers and Carnegies of the past, tasked with shaping future market leaders by refining general AI models into 'cognitive assembly lines' that address specific industry challenges.

Over the next 12 to 18 months, Sequoia plans to focus on five investment themes: persistent memory, seamless communication protocols between AIs, AI voice, end-to-end AI security, and open-source AI.
